one_line: Mauryan emperor Ashoka and his grandson Dasharatha overseeing Ajivika cave dedications in the Barabar and Nagarjuni Hills.

appearance.age_look: Ashoka: mid-40s, broad-shouldered, powerful build; Dasharatha: mid-20s, lean but athletic.
appearance.face: Ashoka has a square jaw, broad nose, deep-set almond eyes, wheatish complexion with a faint vertical scar over his left eyebrow, default calm but intense gaze; Dasharatha has a slightly oval face, sharper nose, bright wide eyes, lighter wheatish tone, default earnest determined expression.
appearance.hair: Both with short, neatly combed black hair parted slightly off-center; Ashoka’s hair is very close-cropped at the sides with a small topknot bound low at the back by a thin gold cord, while Dasharatha’s is a bit longer and smoother, tied into a small low bun with a simple dark red cloth band; no crowns, only a narrow gold diadem band on Ashoka’s forehead and a plain bronze band on Dasharatha’s.
appearance.costume: Ashoka wears a knee-length, sleeveless Mauryan-style royal tunic in deep indigo-dyed cotton, wrapped and belted with a wide saffron cloth sash at the waist, the tunic’s edges trimmed with a subtle double stripe of pale gold; over it he has a soft, unarmoured shoulder mantle of unbleached cream-colored fine cotton draped over his left shoulder and across his chest in Buddhist-influenced simplicity; his lower body is covered by a straight cream dhoti reaching the ankles, pleated in front, paired with plain brown leather sandals; jewellery is modest but regal: a single heavy gold torque at the neck, simple gold stud earrings, and one broad gold bracelet on his right wrist. Dasharatha wears a similar knee-length wrap tunic in rich brick-red cotton, belted with a narrower dark brown leather belt, the tunic edging marked by small alternating black and red woven chevrons characteristic of early northern Indian textiles; he has a lighter, shorter off-white cotton shawl draped around his shoulders, and a tightly wrapped off-white dhoti stopping just above the ankles for ease of movement, with darker brown sandals; his jewellery is simpler—bronze torque, small bronze earrings, and a thin bronze armlet on his left upper arm. Both costumes avoid heavy armor, reflecting a ceremonial inspection of religious caves rather than battlefield gear, and feel authentically Mauryan in draped textiles and restrained ornament.
appearance.signature: Ashoka carries a long, polished wooden staff of authority about shoulder-height, slightly thicker at the top and capped with a small, carved lion-cap finial referencing his imperial lion pillars but rendered in simple wood; Dasharatha carries a rolled palm-leaf document bundle tied with a dark cord, representing his cave inscription grants.

ref_desc: Ashoka and Dasharatha appear together: square-jawed scarred Ashoka with close-cropped hair, gold diadem, indigo tunic, cream dhoti and lion-capped staff beside earnest Dasharatha with low bun, bronze band, brick-red tunic, off-white dhoti and palm-leaf document bundle in indigo, saffron, cream and brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of two Mauryan royals standing side by side in the rocky Barabar–Nagarjuni hills: Ashoka is in his mid-40s, broad and square-jawed with a faint scar above his left eyebrow, short close-cropped black hair and a small low topknot bound by a thin gold cord, a narrow gold diadem band across his forehead, deep-set serious eyes and calm intense expression; he wears a deep indigo knee-length sleeveless wrap tunic edged with pale gold stripes, belted with a wide saffron sash, a soft cream cotton mantle draped over his left shoulder, a straight cream dhoti down to the ankles and plain brown leather sandals, with a single gold torque, simple gold stud earrings and one broad gold bracelet on his right wrist, and he holds a long polished wooden staff capped with a small carved lion finial. Beside him, Dasharatha is in his mid-20s, lean with an oval face, wide determined eyes and neatly tied small low bun of black hair bound with a dark red cloth band and a plain bronze forehead band; he wears a brick-red knee-length wrap tunic edged with tiny black and red woven chevrons, cinched with a narrow dark brown leather belt, a short off-white cotton shawl over his shoulders, a tightly wrapped off-white dhoti ending just above his ankles and darker brown sandals, adorned only with a bronze torque, small bronze earrings and a thin bronze armlet on his left upper arm, holding a rolled palm-leaf document bundle tied with a dark cord. Use a palette dominated by deep indigo, brick red, saffron, cream and brown, and ensure their face shapes, hairstyles, tunics, dhotis, staff and document bundle are clearly visible for consistent redrawing head-to-toe.
